quizQuestion:
Quería que yo {___} con el director.
(She wanted me to talk to the director.)
sentenceTranslation: She wanted me to talk to the director.
explanation: hablara — imperfect subjunctive of hablar (yo). After quería que (past volition trigger), use the imperfect subjunctive. -ar pattern: hablara, hablaras, hablara, habláramos, hablarais, hablaran.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} más despacio, te entendería mejor.
(If you spoke more slowly, I would understand you better.)
sentenceTranslation: If you spoke more slowly, I would understand you better.
explanation: hablaras — imperfect subjunctive of hablar (tú). Type 2 si-clause: si + imperfect subjunctive. The -tú form of the -ra paradigm ends in -aras for -ar verbs.

quizQuestion:
Me pidió que {___} con su jefe.
(He asked me to talk to his boss.)
sentenceTranslation: He asked me to talk to his boss.
explanation: hablara — imperfect subjunctive of hablar (yo/él/ella). After pedirle que (past request), use imperfect subjunctive. Note: yo and él/ella share the same form in the -ra paradigm.

quizQuestion:
Me pidió que {___} a las nueve.
(He asked me to arrive at nine.)
sentenceTranslation: He asked me to arrive at nine.
explanation: llegara — imperfect subjunctive of llegar (yo). After pedir que with a past tense main verb, the subordinate clause uses the imperfect subjunctive. Spelling change: llegar → llegara (g→gu not needed in imperfect subj).

quizQuestion:
Esperaba que {___} a tiempo.
(She hoped you would arrive on time.)
sentenceTranslation: She hoped you would arrive on time.
explanation: llegaras — imperfect subjunctive of llegar (tú). After esperaba que (past emotion/hope), use imperfect subjunctive. Tú form: llegaras.

quizQuestion:
Era necesario que {___} más.
(It was necessary for them to work more.)
sentenceTranslation: It was necessary for them to work more.
explanation: trabajaran — imperfect subjunctive of trabajar (ellos/ellas). After era necesario que (impersonal + past), use imperfect subjunctive. Ellos/ellas form: -aran for -ar verbs.

quizQuestion:
Me alegró que {___} ayer.
(I was glad you called yesterday.)
sentenceTranslation: I was glad you called yesterday.
explanation: llamara — imperfect subjunctive of llamar. After me alegró que (past emotion), use imperfect subjunctive. The yo/él form is llamara.

quizQuestion:
Quería que me {___}.
(I wanted you to help me.)
sentenceTranslation: I wanted you to help me.
explanation: ayudaras — imperfect subjunctive of ayudar (tú). After quería que (past desire), use imperfect subjunctive. The tú form is ayudaras.

quizQuestion:
Era necesario que {___} más.
(It was necessary for you to rest more.)
sentenceTranslation: It was necessary for you to rest more.
explanation: descansaras — imperfect subjunctive of descansar (tú). After era necesario que (impersonal + past), use imperfect subjunctive. Pattern: descansar → descansara, descansaras, descansara...

quizQuestion:
Era importante que {___} para el examen.
(It was important that they study for the exam.)
sentenceTranslation: It was important that they study for the exam.
explanation: estudiaran — imperfect subjunctive of estudiar (ellos/ellas). Impersonal era importante que triggers imperfect subjunctive in a past-framed context. Ellos form: estudiaran.

quizQuestion:
Era importante que {___} más claramente.
(It was important that we speak more clearly.)
sentenceTranslation: It was important that we speak more clearly.
explanation: habláramos — imperfect subjunctive of hablar (nosotros). The nosotros form has a written accent: habláramos. This distinguishes it from the conditional hablaríamos.

quizQuestion:
Esperaba que {___} más duro.
(She hoped you would work harder.)
sentenceTranslation: She hoped you would work harder.
explanation: trabajaras — imperfect subjunctive of trabajar (tú). After esperaba que (past emotion), use the imperfect subjunctive. Tú form: trabajaras.
